Title: Downregulation of N-terminal acetylation triggers ABA-mediated drought responses in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: N-terminal acetyltransferase catalytic subunit NAA10 (NatA complex)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: proteome imprinting, stress-response control, drought adaptation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation of AtNAA10 decreases NatA-dependent N-terminal acetylation, increases free protein N termini, constitutively activates ABA-associated drought responses, and confers strong drought tolerance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant embryo-lethality analysis; complementation with CaMV35S:AtNAA10; amiRNA knockdown; in vitro acetylation assay; co-immunoprecipitation; BiFC; yeast two-hybrid; MS/MS N-terminomics; NBD-Cl free N-termini assay; drought phenotyping
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtNAA10 was identified as the catalytic subunit of the Arabidopsis NatA complex. 2. Loss of AtNAA10 caused embryo lethality, while partial knockdown reduced NatA abundance and plant growth. 3. AtNAA10 depletion specifically reduced acetylation of NatA-type protein N termini after initiator methionine removal. 4. Knockdown plants showed closed stomata, elevated guard-cell ROS, altered root architecture, ABA-marker induction, and marked drought resistance. 5. ABA treatment reduced AtNAA10 transcript/protein abundance and increased free N termini, placing NatA downstream of ABA.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcribed in roots, leaves, stem and flowers
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcribed in roots, leaves, stem and flowers; downregulated by ABA in leaves
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Growth retardation in knockdown lines; embryo lethality in loss-of-function mutants
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Partial downregulation of NatA/NAA10 could be exploited to engineer drought-resistant plants, but strong suppression causes growth retardation and full loss is embryo lethal.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core study focus is protein N-terminal acetylation rather than a classical small-molecule nutrient metabolite; nevertheless NatA depletion downregulated several nutrient-related biosynthetic pathways including flavonoid, phenylpropanoid, sulfate assimilation and α-linolenic acid metabolism. NatA acts downstream of ABA without increasing ABA levels.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Global transcriptome: Affymetrix Arabidopsis Gene 1.0 ST Array; N-terminomics/proteomics by Orbitrap Velos MS/MS; accession NA

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: N-terminal acetyltransferase auxiliary subunit NAA15 (NatA complex ribosome-anchoring subunit)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: proteome imprinting, stress-response control, drought adaptation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation of AtNAA15 destabilizes the NatA complex, lowers NatA-dependent N-terminal acetylation, increases free protein N termini, and induces ABA-like drought responses with strong drought tolerance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant embryo-lethality analysis; amiRNA knockdown; co-immunoprecipitation; BiFC; yeast two-hybrid; ribosome co-fractionation; MS/MS N-terminomics; NBD-Cl free N-termini assay; drought phenotyping
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtNAA15 was identified as the auxiliary/ribosome-associated subunit of the Arabidopsis NatA complex. 2. Loss of AtNAA15 caused embryo lethality, and partial knockdown caused growth retardation while maintaining fertility. 3. AtNAA15 depletion reduced AtNAA10 abundance and NatA substrate acetylation, increasing free N termini. 4. Knockdown plants displayed constitutive drought-adapted traits including stomatal closure, increased guard-cell ROS, higher root-to-shoot ratio, reduced lateral roots and strong drought tolerance. 5. ABA rapidly reduced AtNAA15 transcript/protein abundance, linking NatA downregulation to ABA signaling.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcribed in roots, leaves, stem and flowers
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcribed in roots, leaves, stem and flowers; downregulated by ABA in leaves
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Growth retardation in knockdown lines; embryo lethality in null mutants
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Moderate NatA attenuation via NAA15 downregulation may be useful for drought-tolerance engineering, but severe reduction causes developmental penalties and null alleles are lethal.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Like NAA10, this is not a classical enzyme directly producing a nutrient metabolite; its major metabolism relevance is broad control of proteome acetylation and downstream repression of several biosynthetic pathways under drought/ABA conditions.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Global transcriptome: Affymetrix Arabidopsis Gene 1.0 ST Array; N-terminomics/proteomics by Orbitrap Velos MS/MS; accession NA
